italian: Due biglietti per le mura, per favore.
english: Two tickets for the walls, please.
choicesItalian: Due biglietti per le mura, per favore.
choicesEnglish: Two tickets for the walls, please.
How much is the audio guide?
correctAnswerItalian: Due biglietti per le mura, per favore.
correctAnswerEnglish: Two tickets for the walls, please.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Una richiesta chiara al botteghino.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo due biglietti per le mura.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A clear request at the ticket office.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking for two tickets for the walls.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Una domanda tipica del cassiere.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si riferisce alla tipologia di biglietto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A typical question from the cashier.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it refers to the type of ticket.

english: How much is the combined ticket?
choicesItalian: A che ora chiude il sito?
choicesEnglish: How much is the combined ticket?
What time does the site close?
correctAnswerEnglish: How much is the combined ticket?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Chiedi il prezzo del biglietto unico.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo il costo del cumulativo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! You are asking for the price of the single combined ticket.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking for the cost of the combined ticket.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Chiedi cosa comprende il biglietto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi sapere se il castello è compreso.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! You are asking what the ticket includes.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to know if the castle is included.

english: Is there a discount for students?
choicesEnglish: Is there a discount for students?
correctAnswerEnglish: Is there a discount for students?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Una domanda utile per risparmiare.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo degli sconti per studenti.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A useful question to save money.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking about student discounts.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un'istruzione per orientarsi nel sito.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si parla di seguire le frecce colorate.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! An instruction for navigating the site.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's about following the colored arrows.

english: It is forbidden to climb on the ruins.
choicesEnglish: It is forbidden to climb on the ruins.
It is forbidden to take photos.
It is forbidden to eat here.
It is forbidden to go down from the walls.
correctAnswerEnglish: It is forbidden to climb on the ruins.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Una regola di sicurezza importante.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il divieto riguarda il salire sulle rovine.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! An important safety rule.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the prohibition is about climbing on the ruins.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un avvertimento del personale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa non uscire dal sentiero.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A warning from the staff.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means don't leave the path.

english: What a view from the top!
choicesEnglish: What a view from the top!
correctAnswerEnglish: What a view from the top!

feedback.correctItalian: Molto bene! Un'esclamazione di meraviglia.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, ti stai complimentando per il panorama.
feedback.correctEnglish: Very well! An exclamation of wonder.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are complimenting the view.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Chiedi informazioni sull'epoca del sito.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo se le rovine sono dell'epoca romana.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! You are asking for information about the site's era.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking if the ruins are from the Roman era.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Un modo per chiedere aiuto per l'orientamento.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa che non sapete dove siete.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A way to ask for orientation help.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means you don't know where you are.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Chiedi se la strada è indicata.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi sapere se il sentiero ha delle indicazioni.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! You are asking if the road is indicated.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to know if the path has signs.

italian: L'ultimo ingresso è alle diciassette e trenta.
english: The last entry is at 5:30 PM.
choicesItalian: L'ultimo ingresso è alle diciassette e trenta.
Il tour inizia tra dieci minuti.
choicesEnglish: The last entry is at 5:30 PM.
The tour starts in ten minutes.
correctAnswerItalian: L'ultimo ingresso è alle diciassette e trenta.
correctAnswerEnglish: The last entry is at 5:30 PM.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un'informazione importante sull'orario.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si parla dell'orario limite per entrare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Important information about the schedule.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's about the deadline for entering.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Spiega perché alcune aree non sono visitabili.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa che ci sono dei lavori di riparazione.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Explains why some areas cannot be visited.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means there are repair works.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un cartello che indica un cambio di strada.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa che la strada normale è cambiata.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A sign indicating a change of road.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means the normal road has changed.

italian: La torre è chiusa per restauro.
english: The tower is closed for restoration.
choicesItalian: La torre è chiusa per restauro.
choicesEnglish: The tower is closed for restoration.
correctAnswerItalian: La torre è chiusa per restauro.
correctAnswerEnglish: The tower is closed for restoration.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Spiega perché non si può salire sulla torre.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si parla della torre e dei lavori in corso.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Explains why one cannot go up the tower.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's about the tower and the works in progress.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un motivo di sicurezza per la chiusura.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa che il tetto potrebbe crollare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A safety reason for the closure.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means the roof might collapse.

italian: La vista è quasi la stessa.
english: The view is almost the same.
choicesItalian: La vista è quasi la stessa.
choicesEnglish: The view is almost the same.
correctAnswerItalian: La vista è quasi la stessa.
correctAnswerEnglish: The view is almost the same.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Per rassicurare che la torre alternativa è bella.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa che il panorama non cambia molto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! To reassure that the alternative tower is nice.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means the view doesn't change much.

italian: Dalle mura si gode una vista bellissima.
english: From the walls you can enjoy a beautiful view.
choicesItalian: Dalle mura si gode una vista bellissima.
Dalla torre si vede il mare.
Dal castello si sente la musica.
choicesEnglish: From the walls you can enjoy a beautiful view.
From the tower you can see the sea.
From the castle you can hear music.
From the museum you learn a lot.
correctAnswerItalian: Dalle mura si gode una vista bellissima.
correctAnswerEnglish: From the walls you can enjoy a beautiful view.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Una frase tipica per descrivere il panorama.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, parla del piacere della vista dalle mura.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A typical phrase to describe the view.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it speaks of the pleasure of the view from the walls.

english: Show your ticket at the entrance.
choicesEnglish: Show your ticket at the entrance.
Buy your ticket at the bar.
Look for the ticket in your bag.
correctAnswerEnglish: Show your ticket at the entrance.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Un'istruzione del custode.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa esibire il titolo di viaggio.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! An instruction from the custodian.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means to present your entry document.

english: It dates back to the 15th century.
choicesEnglish: It dates back to the 15th century.
correctAnswerEnglish: It dates back to the 15th century.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Informazione storica sull'età dell'edificio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica quando è stata costruita la struttura.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Historical information about the building's age.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates when the structure was built.

english: Do you see the coat of arms on the portal?
choicesEnglish: Do you see the coat of arms on the portal?
Are you looking for the key in the door?
Are you looking at the photo on the wall?
Do you hear the noise outside?
correctAnswerEnglish: Do you see the coat of arms on the portal?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Attiri l'attenzione su un dettaglio architettonico.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo se nota l'emblema nobiliare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! You are drawing attention to an architectural detail.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking if they notice the noble emblem.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Una bella notizia per il portafoglio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa che non si paga per entrare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Good news for the wallet.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means you don't pay to enter.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Una richiesta di permesso comune.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo se è permesso fotografare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A common request for permission.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking if photography is allowed.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Una regola comune per proteggere l'arte.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa di non usare la luce della fotocamera.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A common rule to protect art.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means not to use the camera light.

english: We have to choose the route.
choicesEnglish: We have to choose the route.
We have to pay the bill.
We have to finish the tour.
correctAnswerEnglish: We have to choose the route.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Bisogna decidere quale itinerario seguire.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa che è necessaria una scelta sulla strada.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A decision must be made on which itinerary to follow.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means a choice about the road is necessary.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Esprimi una preferenza per la durata.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, preferisci l'itinerario più corto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! You are expressing a preference for duration.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you prefer the shorter itinerary.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Chiedi se un'attrazione è compresa nel prezzo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi sapere se la torre è dentro l'offerta.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! You are asking if an attraction is included in the price.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to know if the tower is part of the offer.

italian: Facciamo il completo, ma con calma.

choicesItalian: Facciamo il completo, ma con calma.
choicesEnglish: Let's do the complete one, but slowly.
Let's leave now, we are in a hurry.
Let's take the short one, it's late.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Una proposta per una visita approfondita ma rilassata.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, suggerisci di vedere tutto senza correre.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A proposal for a thorough but relaxed visit.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you suggest seeing everything without rushing.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Una domanda per decidere se farsi accompagnare.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedi se volete una persona che spieghi.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A question to decide whether to be accompanied.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking if you want someone to explain.

choicesEnglish: The audio guide is three euros.
correctAnswerEnglish: The audio guide is three euros.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un'informazione sul prezzo del servizio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si parla del costo dell'audioguida.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Information on the service price.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's about the cost of the audio guide.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Una domanda importante per l'accessibilità.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedi se c'è un mezzo per salire senza scale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! An important question for accessibility.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking if there is a way to go up without stairs.

choicesItalian: Non accessibile in sedia a rotelle.
correctAnswerItalian: Non accessibile in sedia a rotelle.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un'avvertenza per persone con disabilità.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi sapere se puoi sostituire i biglietti.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Quando ti vengono restituiti i soldi.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa restituire il denaro pagato.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Una descrizione storica dell'edificio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si riferisce alla proprietà aristocratica passata.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it refers to the past aristocratic ownership.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Informi sulla presenza di un evento d'arte.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si parla di un'esposizione di foto.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's about a photo exhibition.

correctAnswerEnglish: Follow the arrows for the exit.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, suggerisci come arrivare fuori dal sito.

correctAnswerEnglish: You must go back to the exit.

correctAnswerEnglish: It is forbidden to go down from the walls.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il divieto è sul lasciare le mura scendendo.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa godersi la vista o l'evento.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A polite wish from the staff.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means to enjoy the view or the event.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Chiedi la posizione del sito archeologico.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai cercando l'area degli scavi.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! You are asking for the location of the archaeological site.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means asking for a little time.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo indietro il denaro.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, non è permesso consumare cibo.
